role: A thin, fail-loud HTTP client library that defines the surface's typed data contracts and fetches projection, context, territory, and embedder-layer metadata from the bridge.
capabilities: Defines Projection, ProjPoint, Sector, Edge, BindingRef, ContextBundle, and Territory type contracts
Performs JSON GET requests to /api/projection, /api/context, /api/territory, /api/layers, and /api/layer-dims
Builds query strings for projection parameters
Encodes address parameters for context and territory endpoints
Throws typed ApiError on network, HTTP, and JSON parse failures
substance_why: The file implements complete runtime type contracts for the renderer plus a working fetch helper and five concrete endpoint functions with explicit error handling.
interactions_missing: No request cancellation or AbortController support
No request deduplication or caching layer
No retry/backoff for transient network failures
No pagination or limit controls for context items
No authentication or custom header injection
performance_risks: Every call performs an uncached network fetch with no deduplication, risking redundant requests for repeated addresses or unchanged projection params
No AbortController means in-flight requests may complete after the consumer no longer needs them
fetchProjection serializes all params into a query string on every call without memoization
Large JSON responses are parsed in full and error snippets slice up to 300 characters of text
reuse_verdict_why: The documented contracts and endpoint coverage are central to the redesign and should be preserved, with targeted adaptation for caching, cancellation, and request deduplication.
decomposition_hints: Move type definitions (ProjPoint, Projection, ContextBundle, Territory, etc.) into a dedicated schema/types module
Move generic getJSON and ApiError into a transport/error module
Split endpoint functions by domain: projection.ts, source.ts (context/territory), layers.ts
Add a small request cache/memoization layer alongside the transport module
